title: Ongoing Weakness Outside of China
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
Back in January, we first started stressing in our Weekly Dry Bulk Reports that there was a very real chance that both global iron ore and global coal trade volume could contract this year due to supply issues alone. Later in March (after the onset of the war in Ukraine), we also started stressing that global grain trade volume could end up easily contracting this year due to supply issues as well. From early into the year, major dry bulk commodity trade flow was showing a real chance of actually contracting on a year-on-year basis with the headwinds due simply to supply.
